Design normalized relational database schemas (up to 3NF) and query datasets using structural SQL statements

sub_strands.name: OSI Layering Models and Cryptography
outcomes: Map out data packet movement across the 7 layers of the OSI model architecture
Apply symmetric and asymmetric encryption models to protect local data transmission sockets

sub_strands.name: Lines of Interpenetration and Solid Developments
outcomes: Construct geometric intersection profiles when dissimilar geometric solids (e.g., cylinders intersecting prisms) merge
Generate exact layout surface developments for transition pieces utilized in ventilation ducting design

strands.name: AC Circuit Physics and Semiconductor Logic

sub_strands.name: Alternating Current Vectors and Diode Rectification
outcomes: Calculate impedance, power factor, and resonant frequencies within serial RLC configurations using phasor vectors
Design and solder functional full-wave bridge rectifier circuits using semiconductor diodes and smoothing capacitors

sub_strands.name: Firmware Development and Hardware Interfacing
outcomes: Write clear structural code to program embedded microcontrollers for polling automated sensory telemetry arrays
Interface hardware components using standard serial communication protocols (I2C and SPI links)

sub_strands.name: Partnership Formation and Profit Appropriation
outcomes: Construct Partnership Appropriation Accounts, Capital Accounts, and Current Accounts following partnership deeds
Calculate and account for goodwill allocations, interest on capital, interest on drawings, and partner salaries smoothly

sub_strands.name: Issue of Shares and Debentures Ledger Entries
outcomes: Process ledger entries for the issuance of ordinary shares and preference shares at par, premium, or discount
Construct financial statement disclosures capturing share capital structures and long-term liabilities

sub_strands.name: Circular Flow of Income and Measurement Metrics
outcomes: Model the circular flow of income using multi-sector frameworks (household, firm, government, international trade)
Compute National Income metrics using the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) Expenditure, Income, and Output computation methods

sub_strands.name: Central Banking Mandates and Taxation Systems
outcomes: Analyze monetary policy instruments (open market operations, reserve ratios) deployed to regulate cash reserve matrices
Evaluate progressive, proportional, and regressive tax models alongside public expenditure frameworks

sub_strands.name: Decolonization Movements and Constitutional Changes
outcomes: Analyze the factors that accelerated African nationalism after 1945, highlighting the role of WWII returnees and pan-Africanism
Trace the constitutional milestones leading to Kenya's independence, evaluating the Lancaster House Conferences (1960-1962) frameworks

sub_strands.name: The Legislature and Devolved County Fiscal Frameworks
outcomes: Examine the legislative process, structures, and roles of the National Assembly and Senate in bicameral systems
Critique the revenue allocation mechanisms between national and county governments, analyzing the role of the Commission on Revenue Allocation (CRA)

sub_strands.name: Global Winds, Pressure Belts, and Weather Forecasting Systems
outcomes: Examine the planetary wind systems, planetary pressure belts, and the dynamics of the Inter-Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ)
Interpret complex synoptic weather maps to model short-term regional weather variations

sub_strands.name: Weathering and Mass Wasting
outcomes: Differentiate physical, chemical, and biological weathering processes across diverse climatic zones
Evaluate the causes, categories, and direct environmental hazards associated with rapid mass wasting events

sub_strands.name: Secondary Dominants and Pivot Chord Modulations
outcomes: Identify, resolve, and write secondary dominant chords within major and minor key structures on a grand staff
Compose short harmonic progressions that smoothly modulate to closely related keys using common pivot chords

sub_strands.name: Orchestral Instrument Ranges and Transposition
outcomes: Analyze pitch ranges, clef usage, and acoustic timbres for standard Western orchestral instruments
Transpose musical scores accurately for common transposing instruments including B-flat trumpets, clarinets, and F horns

sub_strands.name: Lighting Design, Stage Acoustics, and Set Architecture
outcomes: Draft comprehensive theatrical lighting plots mapping out fixtures, gels, circuit patches, and illumination angles
Design structural scale models of proscenium and thrust stages implementing specific set architectural designs

sub_strands.name: Impasto, Glazing, and Intaglio Printmaking
outcomes: Execute complex landscape or figurative expressions implementing advanced paint applications such as impasto and transparent glazing layers
Produce fine art prints using traditional intaglio etching or multi-block color woodcut techniques safely

sub_strands.name: Keyframe Animation, Storyboarding, and Vector Assets
outcomes: Construct fluid vector-based 2D animations using keyframe manipulation, ease-in/ease-out curves, and onion-skinning mechanics
Compile professional animatics matching clear timing audio tracks to establish proper kinematic storytelling parameters

sub_strands.name: Aerodynamic Drag, Hydrodynamics, and Magnus Effect
outcomes: Analyze the influence of form drag, skin friction drag, and fluid laminar flows on high-velocity athletic performance
Apply the Magnus Effect to explain the parabolic curve trajectories of spinning projectiles in sports like soccer and tennis

sub_strands.name: VO2 Max Kinetics and Lactate Thresholds
outcomes: Measure and interpret Maximum Oxygen Uptake (VO₂ max) kinetics using standardized continuous laboratory stress protocols
Identify the physiological mechanisms inducing the onset of blood lactate accumulation (OBLA) during progressive exercise intensity increases

sub_strands.name: Project Execution and Portfolio Development
outcomes: Plan and execute a community service project addressing an identified community need
Present a portfolio of evidence demonstrating impact and reflection on the project

sub_strands.name: Indefinite and Definite Integration
outcomes: Derive the integration of polynomial and simple trigonometric functions as the reverse of differentiation
Apply definite integrals to evaluate the exact area bounded under curves and between intersecting boundary lines

sub_strands.name: First-Order Differential Equations
outcomes: Formulate and solve first-order ordinary differential equations utilizing the separation of variables method

sub_strands.name: Imaginary Numbers and Argand Diagrams
outcomes: Perform algebraic operations on complex numbers (z = a + ib) and represent them spatially on Argand diagrams
Convert complex numbers between Cartesian form and polar configuration (r(cosθ + isinθ))

sub_strands.name: Photoelectric Effect and X-Ray Production
outcomes: Analyze the photoelectric equation (E = hf = W₀ + K_max) and solve problems involving work function threshold frequencies
Explain the mechanics of X-ray generation within a Coolidge tube and differentiate continuous vs. characteristic spectra profiles

sub_strands.name: Nuclear Decay Dynamics
outcomes: Formulate balanced nuclear equations for Alpha, Beta, and Gamma decays, and calculate radioisotope half-life decay constants

sub_strands.name: Electrochemical Cells and Standard Electrode Potentials
outcomes: Construct voltaic/galvanic cells, compute standard cell EMF (E°_cell = E°_cathode - E°_anode), and predict reaction spontaneity
Analyze industrial electrolytic processes including the Down's cell extraction of sodium and the electrorefining of copper metal

sub_strands.name: Polymers and Synthetic Macromolecules
outcomes: Differentiate addition and condensation polymerization mechanics, detailing structures for nylon-6,6, Terylene, and polythene
Evaluate environmental impacts of synthetic polymers and research structural biopolymer alternatives

sub_strands.name: Evidence of Evolution and Specialization Drivers
outcomes: Critique evolutionary assertions based on comparative anatomy (homologous vs. analogous organs), embryology, and fossil timelines
Apply the Hardy-Weinberg principle equation (p² + 2pq + q² = 1) to compute allele frequency vectors inside static populations
